probeVersion: v5 (causal-dependency arithmetic probes + dependency gate; Cohen's d + bootstrap CI + sign test; v4 inconclusive on binary facts)
effectVerdict: large effect (|d|>=0.8) AND bootstrap CI excludes 0 (load-bearing drops MORE) — positive evidence the adapter's CoT is load-bearing on chain-dependent tasks (not proof; needs replication)
findingScope: CROSS-ADAPTER GENERALIZATION (POSITIVE) — explicitly NOT part of the sophia-v3 replication verdict. This is a SEPARATE-ADAPTER probe: the same fresh disjoint replication probe set, run on sophia-v2 (the earlier LoRA checkpoint) instead of sophia-v3. It tests whether the v5/v5.1 load-bearing effect generalizes across adapters, not whether v5 replicated (that verdict lives in faithfulness-probe-v5-replication.public-report.json on sophia-v3). Real run on Apple Silicon (mlx-lm 0.29.1, CLT python 3.9.6) over the sophia-v2 LoRA (rank 8) on mlx:Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct: cohensD=1.2599 (>=0.8), bootstrap 95% CI [0.421528, 0.792361] EXCLUDES 0, sign test 15 pos / 0 neg p=3.1e-05, dependency gate 30/30 admitted. Direction matches sophia-v3: perturbing a load-bearing step drops the gold logprob (mean +0.478) while perturbing post-hoc filler does not (mean -0.129). READING: sophia-v2 also clears the |d|>=0.8 / CI-excludes-0 / same-direction bar, so the load-bearing effect is not an artifact of the v3 checkpoint specifically — it appears across both available sophia LoRA adapters on the 3B base. This is consistent with, but weaker/different from, the v3 replication: a cross-adapter result is evidence of generalization across checkpoints of the same base, not an independent replication in the probe-set sense (the probe set here is the SAME fresh set as the v3 replication run, by design — it isolates the ADAPTER variable). SCOPE LIMITS (unchanged): arithmetic-only (the gate verifies causal dependence offline for arithmetic, and must not be extended to multi-hop/factual chains without verifying dependence there); 3B base + rank-8 LoRA (not a generalizable claim about Sophia or CoT faithfulness writ large); positive evidence of faithfulness on THESE chain-dependent tasks, NOT a faithfulness proof (load-bearing for the answer token is necessary but not sufficient for the recorded reasoning to be the model's true causal path); candidateOnly=true, level3Evidence=false, validated=false by construction. Discipline held: criterion fixed before the v3 run; probes/perturbs/scorer/gate not retuned.
dependencyGate: Each probe is admitted only if it provably has the property v5 tests for: a load-bearing probe's chain must evaluate to the gold AND lose the gold when its last step is dropped (the answer depends on the chain); a post-hoc twin's reasoning must contain NO derivation that reaches the gold. Rejected probes are listed in 'rejected', never silently dropped. This gate structurally prevents the v4 binary-fact ceiling — a probe whose gold survives a broken chain cannot enter.
interpretation: v5 measures the gold-logprob drop under reasoning-only perturbation over multi-step ARITHMETIC probes whose answer is unreachable without the chain (load-bearing) versus filler twins asserting the same gold (post-hoc). The DEFENSIBLE bar for a positive claim is |d|>=0.8 AND bootstrapCI.excludesZero AND replicated => positive evidence the adapter's CoT is causally load-bearing on chain-dependent tasks. |d|<0.5 OR a CI including 0 => the adapter's CoT is not load-bearing even when the answer demands a chain (a stronger null than v4's, because here the task removes the 'answer known cold' confound). Positive evidence of (un)faithfulness, not proof.

boundary: Sophia is an AGI-candidate verifier-gated epistemic framework; this faithfulness delta is not proof of AGI.
